comment: Canonical curated image library. Every entry: exact verified year, license audited against the Wikimedia Commons API (verified block), one-line reveal blurb, attribution line, difficulty 1 (easiest) to 5. Images referenced by Commons filename; URLs are built by the generator (Special:FilePath for now, CDN base later).

images.blurb: Orville Wright pilots the first powered flight at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, while Wilbur runs alongside.
images.credit: John T. Daniels / Library of Congress — public domain

images.blurb: Residents of Sacramento Street watch the fires after the San Francisco earthquake.
RMS Titanic departs Southampton on her maiden — and only — voyage.

images.commonsFile: Charles Lindbergh and the Spirit of Saint Louis (Crisco restoration, with wings).jpg
images.blurb: Charles Lindbergh with the Spirit of St. Louis, the year of the first solo transatlantic flight.
Dorothea Lange's "Migrant Mother": Florence Owens Thompson in a pea pickers' camp in Nipomo, California.
images.credit: Dorothea Lange / Farm Security Administration — public domain

images.blurb: The airship Hindenburg burns while landing at Naval Air Station Lakehurst, New Jersey.

images.commonsFile: Martin Luther King - March on Washington.jpg
images.blurb: Martin Luther King Jr. at the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom.
images.credit: Rowland Scherman / U.S. Information Agency — public domain

images.blurb: "Earthrise," taken from lunar orbit during Apollo 8 — the first crewed mission to leave Earth orbit.
Buzz Aldrin on the Sea of Tranquility, photographed by Neil Armstrong during Apollo 11.
Elvis Presley meets Richard Nixon in the Oval Office — the National Archives' most-requested photograph.
images.credit: Ollie Atkins / White House — public domain

verified.artist: Ollie Atkins, chief White House photographer

images.blurb: "The Blue Marble," taken by the crew of Apollo 17 — the last crewed lunar mission — on the way to the Moon.
Times Square in high summer, photographed for the EPA's Documerica project.
images.credit: Dan McCoy / EPA Documerica, National Archives — public domain

images.commonsFile: MANY SUBWAY CARS IN NEW YORK CITY HAVE BEEN SPRAY-PAINTED BY VANDALS - NARA - 548260.jpg
images.blurb: Riders in a spray-painted New York subway car, photographed for the EPA's Documerica project.
images.credit: Erik Calonius / EPA Documerica, National Archives — public domain

images.commonsFile: "POOL IT" SIGN NORTH OF VANCOUVER, WASHINGTON, WAS A REMINDER THAT THE GASOLINE SHORTAGE WAS NOT OVER IN MARCH, 1974... - NARA - 555504.jpg
images.blurb: A carpooling appeal near Vancouver, Washington, as the first oil crisis dragged on.
images.credit: David Falconer / EPA Documerica, National Archives — public domain

images.blurb: Voyager 2 leaves Earth aboard a Titan IIIE — carrying the Golden Record toward interstellar space.
Mount St. Helens erupts on the morning of May 18 — the deadliest volcanic event in U.S. history.
Space Shuttle Columbia lifts off on STS-1, the first flight of the Shuttle program.

images.commonsFile: Sally Ride, America's first woman astronaut communicates with ground controllers from the flight deck - NARA - 541940.jpg
images.blurb: Sally Ride on Challenger's flight deck during STS-7 — America's first woman in space.
Opening ceremony of the Summer Olympics at the Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum.
Space Shuttle Challenger breaks apart 73 seconds after launch.
images.credit: NASA / Kennedy Space Center — public domain

images.commonsFile: President Ronald Reagan Making His Berlin Wall Speech at Brandenburg Gate West Berlin - DPLA - dce9b53e6ef9b7e01d184ce61f78871b.jpg
images.blurb: "Tear down this wall" — Ronald Reagan speaks at the Brandenburg Gate in West Berlin.
images.credit: White House Photographic Office / DPLA — public domain

images.commonsFile: F-14A VF-114 over burning Kuwaiti oil well 1991.JPEG
images.blurb: A U.S. Navy F-14 over Kuwait's burning oil wells in the months after the Gulf War.
images.credit: Lt. Steve Gozzo, U.S. Navy — public domain

images.commonsFile: Unity Module in the Payload Bay for STS-88 - GPN-2000-000825.jpg
images.blurb: The International Space Station begins: the Unity node rides in Endeavour's payload bay before mating with Zarya.

images.commonsFile: Mike Melvill upon the completion of mission 15P the first private trip into Space photo Don Ramey Logan.jpg
images.blurb: Test pilot Mike Melvill celebrates atop SpaceShipOne after the first privately funded human spaceflight.
New Orleans under water in the days after Hurricane Katrina.
images.credit: Cmdr. Mark Moran / NOAA — public domain

images.blurb: The crowd on the National Mall for Barack Obama's first inauguration.

images.commonsFile: Deepwater Horizon offshore drilling unit on fire 2010.jpg
images.blurb: Fireboats battle the Deepwater Horizon blaze in the Gulf of Mexico.

images.commonsFile: Occupy Wall Street Crowd Size 2011 Shankbone.JPG
images.blurb: Occupy Wall Street gathers near Zuccotti Park on the movement's first day.

images.commonsFile: 2012 Summer Olympics opening ceremony, Olympic rings.jpg
images.blurb: The rings are forged: opening ceremony of the London Summer Olympics.
Pluto in true color, captured by New Horizons during humanity's first flyby of the dwarf planet.
Babe Ruth in his first season as a Yankee — the year the home run changed baseball.

images.commonsFile: Into the Jaws of Death 23-0455M edit.jpg
images.blurb: "Into the Jaws of Death": U.S. troops wade toward Omaha Beach on the morning of D-Day.
images.credit: Robert F. Sargent, U.S. Coast Guard — public domain

images.blurb: V-J Day in Times Square: a sailor's kiss as news of Japan's surrender breaks.
images.credit: Victor Jorgensen, U.S. Navy — public domain

images.blurb: A U.S. Marine Pershing tank in the streets of Seoul during the Korean War.
images.credit: U.S. Navy / Naval History and Heritage Command — public domain

images.blurb: The Space Needle rises over the Century 21 Exposition — the Seattle World's Fair.
UH-1D helicopters lift infantry during Operation Wahiawa, northeast of Cu Chi, South Vietnam.
images.credit: James K. F. Dung, SFC, U.S. Army — public domain

images.blurb: Space Shuttle Atlantis docked to Russia's Mir station, photographed from a departing Soyuz.
The Women's March on Washington, the day after the presidential inauguration.
Falcon Heavy's first flight leaves Pad 39A — with a Roadster for a payload.
Times Square, deserted at midday in the first spring of the pandemic.
Perseverance's selfie with the Ingenuity helicopter at Jezero Crater, Mars.
